query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are some of the **top AI headlines as of Thursday, April 9, 2026**:

- **Anthropic previews “Mythos” in a cybersecurity push.** TechCrunch reported on April 7 that Anthropic debuted a preview of a new model called **Mythos** as part of a security-focused initiative, signaling continued competition around frontier model launches and enterprise/security use cases. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-mythos-ai-model-preview-security/?utm_source=openai))

- **Microsoft expands its in-house AI model strategy.** TechCrunch reported on April 2 that Microsoft introduced **three new foundational models**, a sign it is building more of its own AI stack even while maintaining ties to OpenAI. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/02/microsoft-takes-on-ai-rivals-with-three-new-foundational-models/?utm_source=openai))

- **Microsoft is taking over a major Texas AI data center expansion after OpenAI stepped back.** AP reported last week that Microsoft assumed control of a Texas data center construction project that OpenAI declined to pursue, underscoring how AI infrastructure and cloud relationships are shifting as model makers race for compute. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/f4f74c3a4617d8cfab5b933fc31ccc6e?utm_source=openai))

- **OpenAI leadership changes are in focus.** TechCrunch’s April 3 coverage said OpenAI made an executive shuffle that gives COO **Brad Lightcap** a new role leading “special projects,” a notable management move at one of the industry’s most influential companies. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/03/...?utm_source=openai))

- **Big platforms say Anthropic’s Claude remains available to most customers despite Pentagon tensions.** TechCrunch reported on March 6 that Microsoft, Google, and Amazon said Anthropic’s models would remain available to non-defense customers, highlighting the growing policy and national-security dimension of AI competition. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/06/microsoft-anthropic-claude-remains-available-to-customers-except-the-defense-department/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **news briefing with 1–2 sentence summaries per story plus direct publication names and dates**, or narrow it to **OpenAI / Google / Microsoft / regulation / AI startups** only.
